mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend remains upward.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS for 2006-05 was 4.94 percent
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and tighter household credit conditions, reducing expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST for 2006-04 was 1849 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST breakdowns are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with strong PAYEMS growth signals a tight labor market that raises wage inflation pressure and expected policy restraint, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.0% and PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change above 150 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE for 2006-05 was 4.6 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE strong-PAYEMS condition has a 20-day forward hit rate for negative S&P 500 returns below 45% or if its mean return exceeds the sample baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding real output and improving operating leverage, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends are not followed by higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO trends, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL signals restrictive policy in response to inflation, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-FEDFUNDS high-CPI condition is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if its average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive two-quarter growth in GDPC1 signals broad real demand expansion, improving revenue expectations and risk appetite, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 vintage observation was for 2006-01
failure_condition: Reject if positive two-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, lowering expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 3-month prior level by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE for 2006-05 was 4.6 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the triggered average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Falling HOUST while FEDFUNDS rises identifies monetary transmission through housing credit, reducing construction activity and household wealth, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month change below -5% and FEDFUNDS 6-month change above +0.50 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2006-06-15 monetary policy was in a tightening phase
regime_hint: late-cycle housing stress under monetary tightening
failure_condition: Reject if the joint falling-HOUST rising-FEDFUNDS condition does not predict lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than either single condition alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative.
